title: config.root is an OPTIONAL override; the .pi-context.json pointer is the substrate-dir SoT; the packaged conception ships no root
context: DEC-0015 mandates no default substrate dir (resolveContextDir hard-throws; /project init requires an explicit dir). But config.schema.json required 'root' and the packaged conception (samples/conception.json:3) shipped root: '.project' — a hidden shipped substrate-dir default (FGAP-094). adoptConception (project-context.ts:436) already overwrites conception.root with path.relative(cwd, contextDirAbs) on every adopted config, and projectRoot (project-context.ts:250) already falls back to resolveContextDir(cwd) — the .pi-context.json bootstrap pointer — when root is absent. config.root is therefore redundant with the pointer today. loadConfig/writeConfig validate against the bundled schema only (bundledSchemaPath('config')); config is not wired through validateBlockWithMigration, so a schema version bump triggers no migration. The forcing question: should the config contract keep 'root' required (and so force the template to ship a concrete default), or make it an optional override so the conception ships no root?
decision: Make config.root an OPTIONAL schema field. The .pi-context.json bootstrap pointer is the single source of truth for the substrate-dir name; config.root is an optional relocation override, set by adoptConception at accept-all from the init-chosen dir, and resolved-via-pointer when absent. The packaged conception is a TEMPLATE and ships no root. This enforces DEC-0015 on the shipped artifact: no default substrate dir is shipped, hidden or otherwise. config.schema.json version bumps 1.3.0 -> 1.4.0. User-story (affirmed by user authority at enactment): I name my substrate dir once; the framework honors that name everywhere and ships no competing default.
consequences: config.schema.json required[] drops 'root'; root description rewritten to optional-override-resolved-via-pointer; version 1.4.0
samples/conception.json ships no root key; samplesCatalog() / SamplesCatalog no longer surface a root
Hand-authored configs may omit root; projectRoot resolves the substrate via the pointer (already the behavior — now contractually allowed)
Relaxation only: existing configs that carry root remain valid; no migration needed (config not migration-wired)
The frozen .project/schemas/config.schema.json copy is unchanged (DEC-0036) and is not used by loadConfig (bundled schema is)
Does not rename .project anywhere — the residual 'project' naming/location debt remains FGAP-074 / DEC-0036 cutover territory
